caption: The video presents a historical overview of Maggie Boyd, featuring archival documents and imagery. It begins with an image of a weathered blue leather diary next to a handwritten entry from "TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 4, 1873," which reads, "Study and write, study and write, what monotony! Sometimes I get tired." The scene then transitions to an "Official Trustees Record, June 25, 1873," showing a formal handwritten ledger. The sequence continues with a photograph of a brick building, identified by text as "Boyd Hall, Ladies Dormitory, Ohio University, Athens," accompanied by a description stating it was named in her honor and later torn down for Alden Library. Finally, the video shows a 1917 newspaper clipping from "THE OHIO ALUMINUS" titled "Diary Indicates First Co-Ed Was Noticed on OU Campus," featuring a portrait of "Margaret Boyd, The Trustee."
